description_long: This object is a historical cannon mounted on a wooden wheeled carriage, reminiscent of artillery used in the 17th to 19th centuries. The cannon barrel is made of dark, weathered metal, featuring a flared muzzle and reinforcing rings along its length. The carriage is constructed from rough, aged wood, with four solid wooden wheels and metal fittings for structural support. The cannon is fixed atop the carriage, with trunnions resting in side supports, and a rear handle or lever for aiming adjustments. The overall appearance suggests a robust, utilitarian design intended for battlefield deployment. The asset shows signs of wear, rust, and patina, indicating extensive use or age.
description: A weathered metal cannon mounted on a wooden wheeled carriage, featuring a flared muzzle and aged, robust construction.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular muzzle of the cannon and the symmetrical arrangement of the wooden carriage and wheels.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full length of the cannon barrel, the wooden carriage, and all four wheels.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the carriage, the breech of the cannon, and the rear handle or lever.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the cannon barrel, carriage, and wheels from the other profile.
attribute_weapon_mounts: One fixed cannon barrel mounted centrally on the wooden carriage.
